meta.ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances
meta.project_subject: Work Order for extension of SCDPM PMU (01.04.2025 to 31.03.2026)
meta.deliverables: The deliverables include monitoring progress on the Special campaign/SCDPM Portal, data analytics of portal data, drafting agenda for monthly review meetings, preparing presentations and reports on ministry updates, analyzing best practices monthly, preparing monthly Secretariat Reforms reports, creating assessment reports and presentations for the Special Campaign, providing insights on e-Office Analytics via dashboard, and preparing detailed chapters on best practices and focused ministry analysis.

content.full_text: The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions has issued a work order to the Quality Council of India (QCI) for extending the SCDPM PMU from 01.04.2025 to 31.03.2026. The project involves a team of three QCI professionals who will monitor the Special Campaign for Disposal of Pendency Matters (SCDPM) portal, perform data analytics, draft meeting agendas, prepare presentations and reports, analyze best practices, and provide e-Office analytics insights. The total cost is Rs. 65,52,000/- for one year, with professionals required to have relevant qualifications including at least one with core expertise in data analytics from a Tier 1 college. Payment will be made monthly based on actual attendance, and services may be discontinued if unsatisfactory.
